params.name: updateHistory
params.description: Whether the project in which the issue is created is added to the user's Recently viewed project list, as shown under Projects in Jira.

items.docs: Creates an issue or a sub-task from a JSON representation. The fields that can be set on an issue depend on the connected Jira instance configuration, the project, and the issue type. You can determine appropriate field values using the Create Issue Metadata endpoint.

params.name: issueIdOrKey
params.description: The ID or key of the issue (e.g., 10001 or PROJ-123).

params.name: fields
params.description: A list of fields to return for the issue. Use it to retrieve a subset of fields. Allowed values include any field name, *all for all fields, *navigable for navigable fields.

params.name: expand
params.description: Use expand to include additional information in the response. Accepted values: renderedFields, names, schema, transitions, operations, editmeta, changelog, versionedRepresentations, fields.

params.name: properties
params.description: A list of issue properties to return for the issue. By default no properties are returned.

params.name: fieldsByKeys
params.description: Whether fields in fields are referenced by keys rather than IDs.

items.docs: Returns the details for an issue. The issue is identified by its ID or key. The returned issue includes all fields, comment count, names, schema, transitions, editmeta, changelog, and rendered fields.

params.name: issueIdOrKey
params.description: The ID or key of the issue (e.g., 10001 or PROJ-123).

params.name: notifyUsers
params.description: Whether a notification email about the issue update is sent to all watchers. Requires administer Jira global permission.

params.name: overrideScreenSecurity
params.description: Whether screen security is overridden to enable hidden fields to be edited. Requires administer Jira global permission.

params.name: overrideEditableFlag
params.description: Whether the non-editable flag is overridden to enable uneditable fields to be edited. Requires administer Jira global permission.

items.docs: Edits an issue. A transition may be applied and issue properties updated as part of the edit. The editable fields depend on the connected Jira instance configuration, the project, and the issue type.

params.name: issueIdOrKey
params.description: The ID or key of the issue (e.g., 10001 or PROJ-123).

params.name: deleteSubtasks
params.description: Whether the issue's sub-tasks are also deleted.

items.docs: Deletes an issue. An issue can only be deleted if it has no sub-tasks, unless deleteSubtasks is set to true.

params.name: issueIdOrKey
params.description: The ID or key of the issue (e.g., 10001 or PROJ-123).

params.name: expand
params.description: Use expand to include additional information about transitions in the response. Accepted values: transitions.fields.

params.name: transitionId
params.description: The ID of the transition.

params.name: skipRemoteOnlyCondition
params.description: Whether transitions with the condition Hide From User Condition are included in the response.

params.name: includeUnavailableTransitions
params.description: Whether details of transitions that fail a condition are included.

items.docs: Returns either all transitions or a transition that can be performed by the user on an issue, based on the issue's status.

params.name: issueIdOrKey
params.description: The ID or key of the issue (e.g., 10001 or PROJ-123).

items.docs: Performs an issue transition and, optionally, updates the fields and adds a comment. The fields that can be set depend on the transition and the connected Jira instance configuration.

params.name: issueIdOrKey
params.description: The ID or key of the issue (e.g., 10001 or PROJ-123).

params.name: startAt
params.description: The index of the first item to return in a page of results (page offset).

params.name: maxResults
params.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.

params.name: orderBy
params.description: The order of comments returned. Accepted values: created (ascending by default), -created (descending).

params.name: expand
params.description: Use expand to include additional information about comments in the response. Accepted values: renderedBody.

items.docs: Returns all comments for an issue. Comments are ordered by created date in ascending order by default.

params.name: issueIdOrKey
params.description: The ID or key of the issue (e.g., 10001 or PROJ-123).

params.name: expand
params.description: Use expand to include additional information about comments in the response. Accepted values: renderedBody.

items.docs: Adds a comment to an issue. The comment body uses Atlassian Document Format (ADF).

params.name: expand
params.description: Use expand to include additional information in the response. Accepted values: description, issueTypes, lead, projectKeys, url.

params.name: recent
params.description: Returns projects the user has accessed in the last given number of projects, up to 20.

params.name: properties
params.description: A list of project properties to return for each project.

items.docs: Returns all projects visible to the user. Deprecated, use Get projects paginated that supports search and pagination.

params.name: startAt
params.description: The index of the first item to return in a page of results (page offset).

params.name: maxResults
params.description: The maximum number of items to return per page.

params.name: orderBy
params.description: Order the results by a field. Accepted values: category, key, name, owner, issueCount, lastIssueUpdatedTime, archivedDate. Prefix with + or - for ascending/descending.

params.name: query
params.description: Filter results using a literal string match on project name or key.

params.name: typeKey
params.description: The type key of the project. Accepted values: business, service_desk, software.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The ID of the project category to filter by.

params.name: action
params.description: Filter results by what the user has permission to do. Accepted values: view, browse, edit, create.

params.name: expand
params.description: Use expand to include additional information in the response. Accepted values: description, projectKeys, lead, issueTypes, url, insight.

params.name: status
params.description: The status of the project. Accepted values: live, archived, deleted.

items.docs: Returns a paginated list of projects visible to the user. This operation can be filtered by project type, category, and action.

params.name: projectIdOrKey
params.description: The ID or key of the project (e.g., 10000 or PROJ).

params.name: expand
params.description: Use expand to include additional information in the response. Accepted values: description, issueTypes, lead, projectKeys, url, insight.

params.name: properties
params.description: A list of project properties to return for the project.

items.docs: Returns the project details for a project. The returned project includes additional information such as issue types, components, versions, and roles.

info.name: Jira Get All Statuses for Project

params.name: projectIdOrKey
params.description: The ID or key of the project (e.g., 10000 or PROJ).

items.docs: Returns the valid statuses for a project. The statuses are grouped by issue type, listing the allowed statuses for each issue type in the project.

info.name: Jira Search for Issues Using Jql (get)

params.name: jql
params.value: project = PROJ AND status = Open ORDER BY created DESC
params.description: The JQL query string. For example: project = HSP AND issuetype = Bug. See JQL documentation for syntax.

params.name: startAt
params.description: The index of the first item to return in a page of results (page offset).

params.name: maxResults
params.description: The maximum number of items to return per page. The maximum allowed value is determined by the Jira instance configuration, typically 100.

params.name: validateQuery
params.description: Whether to validate the JQL query and how to handle validation results. Accepted values: strict, warn, none.

params.name: fields
params.description: A list of fields to return for each issue. Use *all for all fields, *navigable for navigable fields only.

params.name: expand
params.description: Use expand to include additional information about issues in the response. Accepted values: renderedFields, names, schema, transitions, operations, editmeta, changelog, versionedRepresentations.

params.name: properties
params.description: A list of issue properties to return for each issue.

params.name: fieldsByKeys
params.description: Whether fields in fields are referenced by keys rather than IDs.

items.docs: Searches for issues using JQL. If the JQL query expression is too large to be encoded as a query parameter, use the POST version of this resource. Sorting is supported via the ORDER BY clause in JQL.

info.name: Jira Search for Issues Using Jql (post)

items.docs: Searches for issues using JQL. Use this endpoint when the JQL query expression is too large to send as a query parameter. This provides the same functionality as the GET version.

info.name: Jira Get All Issue Types for User

items.docs: Returns all issue types visible to the user. The list of issue types returned depends on the user's permissions.
Returns the list of all issue priorities.
